title: Stealth Companies Pitch AI Governance Solutions - LinkedIn
full_text:
Stealth Companies Pitch AI Governance Solutions
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Joseph Cooper
2mo
Report this post
Since being back i've listened to 3 different stealth companies all pitching the same thing.
Securing agent interactions at runtime, done by way of automated red teaming for agents
Things are heating up and will be very interesting to see how this AI Governance space pans out.
20
2 Comments
Like
Comment
Share
Copy
LinkedIn
Facebook
Mike Privette
2mo
Report this comment
Agreed, and I've had similar conversations.
It has been an incredibly fast-moving space, and as the capabilities and integration points of agents and AI systems have grown, the need for observability, runtime security checks, and adversarial testing has become even more important.
Like
Reply
6 Reactions
7 Reactions
Zsolt N.
2mo
Report this comment
Does it still make sense after Bloomberg article yesterday? No way automated red teaming is fast enough to counter Mythos... Fyi https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-21/anthropic-s-mythos-model-is-being-accessed-by-unauthorized-users

Reply

2 Reactions

2mo
Report this post
Most businesses don't need one AI agent.
They need a team of agents, each running a different workflow — and a mission control system to monitor everything.
Tina Huang just broke down her complete OpenClaw setup: 6 agents

Terry Dortch
2mo
Report this post
With all the news around AI and now everyone is finally conceding to the fact that the tools and processes we are using today are not working and will have even less affect in the future as the bad guy becomes stronger, faster, and more nimble than the traditional DETECT-RESPONSE platforms!!!!
It is imperative that we take a more aggressive OFFENSIVE approach and actually try to stop the attack before it ever gets into the environment we are protecting.
Below is a short diagram that shows just how we at ARMP powered by Ridgeback Network Defense set ourselves up to stop the attack and we do so at level 2 long before any platform or process ever sees the attack!!!

Quantum Synapse AI
72 followers
2mo
Report this post
Attackers are not guessing anymore. They continuously simulate, map attack paths, identify exploit routes, and stress-test systems using automation and AI.
The organizations that meet with annual penetration tests are not managing risk. They are managing a countdown.
Quantum Strike shifts the posture entirely; continuous adversarial simulation that behaves like a real attacker surfaces real weaknesses and delivers intelligence that leadership can act on before exploitation occurs.
Offense, when governed and controlled, is what strengthens defense permanently.
#QuantumStrike #AdversarialSimulation #CyberIntelligence #EnterpriseResilience

𝗔𝗸𝘁𝗼 partners with Portkey , TrueFoundry , Arcade.dev , LiteLLM AI Gateway , and LangChain to bring real-time, in-line security to enterprise AI agent and MCP deployments.
Together, these partnerships help defend against prompt injection, uncontrolled tool access, and sensitive data leakage, right where teams build and run agents, making runtime security the default, not an afterthought.
🔒 🐬 𝗦𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆-𝗯𝘆-𝗱𝗲𝗳𝗮𝘂𝗹𝘁 𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗰𝗸.

EnGenAI
572 followers
1mo
Edited
Report this post
Every AI tool gives agents tools.
None give you control over HOW agents think.
EnGenAI has three layers of behavioural steering:
→ Layer 0 (Floor): organisation-wide locked policies. Unbypassable.
→ Layer 1 (Structural): 7-state lifecycle, Impact Gate, anti-loop protection.
→ Layer 2 (Prompt): dynamic identity, memory context injection.
→ Layer 3 (Runtime): creativity and compliance dials, tunable on the fly.
Three layers of steering. Zero layers of guesswork.
No competitor has all of this.
#EnGenAI #AIAgents #AIGovernance Hans van Loon
…more

James B.
1mo
Report this post
AI is an exciting field, but many people remain unaware of the risks associated with its deployment and usage. A significant concern is Prompt Injection, which can lead to various harms.
This is a great video highlighting what Prompt Injection is, the potential risks it poses, and how CrowdStrike is addressing this issue: https://lnkd.in/g_xWq3mS
…more
Stopping AI Agent Attacks: How Falcon AIDR Blocks Prompt Injection
https://www.youtube.com/
22
1 Comment
Like
Comment
Share
Copy
LinkedIn
Facebook
To view or add a comment, sign in
Jon W.
1mo
Report this post
Once AI systems can create real-world consequence at machine speed, post-hoc governance collapses.
And once that realization lands, everyone eventually runs into the same physics:
* centralized governance is too slow
* humans cannot remain in-path
* observability is not prevention
* logs are not proof
* policy is not enforcement
* orchestration is not authority
That forces architectures toward some form of:
* local execution constraint
* deterministic admissibility
* runtime verification
* independent evidence
* bounded authority
* cryptographic proof
* hardware-aware enforcement

To view or add a comment, sign in
Hoàng Lê
1mo
Edited
Report this post
AI agents act at machine speed and don't have "consequences for bad action," which is why runtime identity is the new must-have control plane.
Ping Identity CEO Andre Durand joined NYSE at RSAC to talk about how we’re building the guardrails for Agentic AI and securing the non-human workforce, and shifting the security boundary to the exact moment of action.
Full interview: https://ow.ly/hW0y30sVJmT

Attackers are not guessing anymore. They continuously simulate, map attack paths, identify exploit routes, and stress-test systems using automation and AI.
The organizations that meet with annual penetration tests are not managing risk. They are managing a countdown.
Quantum Strike shifts the posture entirely; continuous adversarial simulation that behaves like a real attacker surfaces real weaknesses and delivers intelligence that leadership can act on before exploitation occurs.
Offense, when governed and controlled, is what strengthens defense permanently.

→ Layer 2 (Prompt): dynamic identity, memory context injection.
→ Layer 3 (Runtime): creativity and compliance dials, tunable on the fly.
Three layers of steering. Zero layers of guesswork.

Jon W.

Once AI systems can create real-world consequence at machine speed, post-hoc governance collapses.
And once that realization lands, everyone eventually runs into the same physics:
* centralized governance is too slow
* humans cannot remain in-path
* observability is not prevention
* logs are not proof
* policy is not enforcement
* orchestration is not authority
That forces architectures toward some form of:
* local execution constraint
* deterministic admissibility
* runtime verification
* independent evidence
* bounded authority
* cryptographic proof
* hardware-aware enforcement
